factors.summary: Capital expenditures are consistent with prior years.

evidence.text: Capital expenditures of $40.1, $28.5 and $24.7 were incurred during 2022, 2021 and 2020, respectively. Capitalized software expenditures of $30.2, $29.7 and $17.7 were incurred during 2022, 2021 and 2020, respectively. Capital expenditures and capitalized software expenditures were relatively consistent in 2022 as compared to 2021 and 2020. In the future, we expect the aggregate of capital expenditures and capitalized software expenditures as a percentage of annual net revenues to be between 1.0% and 1.5%.
evidence.subsection: All currency amounts are in millions

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Capital expenditures are consistent with prior years, indicating stability.

evidence.text: Total debt excluding unamortized debt issuance costs was $6,700.3 at December 31, 2022 (29.5% of total capital) compared to $7,970.3 at December 31, 2021 (40.8% of total capital). Our total debt decreased at December 31, 2022 compared to December 31, 2021, due primarily to repayments of $800.0 for our senior notes and net repayments of $470.0 on our unsecured credit facility.
evidence.subsection: All currency amounts are in millions

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Total debt decreased, indicating strengthening debt leverage.

factors.summary: Input costs are rising, particularly in Technology Enabled Products, but the company is managing through pricing and productivity improvements.

evidence.text: Gross margin decreased to 56.9% in the year ended December 31, 2022, as compared to 59.2% in the year ended December 31, 2021, due primarily to higher material, component and freight costs as our businesses navigate the widespread global supply chain challenges. SG&A expenses as a percentage of net revenues decreased to 23.8% in the year ended December 31, 2022, as compared to 25.7% in the year ended December 31, 2021 due primarily to improved operating leverage on higher organic sales.
evidence.subsection: Year Ended December 31, 2022 Compared to Year Ended December 31, 2021

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Input costs are rising, despite management efforts, indicating a headwind.

factors.summary: Foreign exchange movements and input costs are key sensitivities impacting near-term results.

evidence.text: Foreign Exchange (1.3) (1.3) (0.9) (1.2) Organic Revenue Growth 7.5% 12.7% 9.8% 9.4% ... Gross margin decreased to 56.9% in the year ended December 31, 2022, as compared to 59.2% in the year ended December 31, 2021, due primarily to higher material, component and freight costs as our businesses navigate the widespread global supply chain challenges.
evidence.subsection: Year Ended December 31, 2022 Compared to Year Ended December 31, 2021

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Foreign exchange and input costs are key risks impacting near-term results.

factors.summary: Demand is accelerating across all segments, with net revenue growth increasing from 20.2% in 2021 to 11.1% in 2022. The evidence shows consistent organic revenue growth across all segments, though the growth rate slowed slightly from 2021 to 2022.

evidence.text: Net revenues for the year ended December 31, 2021 were $4,833.8 as compared to $4,022.4 for the year ended December 31, 2020, an increase of 20.2%
evidence.subsection: Year Ended December 31, 2021 Compared to Year Ended December 31, 2020

evidence.text: Net revenues for the year ended December 31, 2022 were $5,371.8 as compared to $4,833.8 for the year ended December 31, 2021, an increase of 11.1%
evidence.subsection: Year Ended December 31, 2022 Compared to Year Ended December 31, 2021

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Demand is accelerating with increasing net revenue growth.

factors.summary: Order backlog has consistently grown over the past two years, with a 22.0% increase from 2020 to 2021 and a 25.3% increase from 2021 to 2022. The growth has been driven primarily by organic expansion, with additional contributions from acquisitions, though there was a minor offset from foreign exchange impacts.

evidence.text: Order backlog increased 25.3% to $2,912.6 at December 31, 2022 as compared to $2,325.1 at December 31, 2021. Organic growth in backlog was 18% and acquisitions contributed 8% which was partially offset by foreign exchange impact of 1%.
evidence.subsection: Year Ended December 31, 2021 Compared to Year Ended December 31, 2020

evidence.text: Order backlog is equal to our remaining performance obligations expected to be recognized within the next 12 months as discussed in Note 1 of the Notes to Consolidated Financial Statements. Backlog increased 22.0% to $2,325.1 at December 31, 2021 as compared to $1,905.5 at December 31, 2020, with the increase driven primarily by organic growth.
evidence.subsection: Year Ended December 31, 2022 Compared to Year Ended December 31, 2021

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Order backlog has consistently grown, indicating strong demand and future revenue.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth has remained strong and positive, with evidence of acceleration across all segments in the most recent year compared to the prior year. The growth was broad-based and driven by multiple market segments, reflecting the company's ability to leverage its operations and expand its offerings.

evidence.text: The growth of 9.1% in organic revenues was broad-based across the segment led by our businesses serving the government contracting, acute healthcare and legal markets.
evidence.subsection: Year Ended December 31, 2021 Compared to Year Ended December 31, 2020

evidence.text: The growth of 7.5% in organic revenues was broad-based across the segment led by our businesses serving the property and casualty insurance, acute healthcare, and government contracting markets.
evidence.subsection: Year Ended December 31, 2022 Compared to Year Ended December 31, 2021

evidence.text: The growth of 12.7% in organic revenues was led by our network software businesses serving the freight match, life insurance, and media and entertainment markets.
evidence.subsection: Year Ended December 31, 2022 Compared to Year Ended December 31, 2021

evidence.text: The growth of 9.8% in organic revenues was primarily due to our water meter technology business and medical products businesses.
evidence.subsection: Year Ended December 31, 2022 Compared to Year Ended December 31, 2021

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Organic revenue growth is strong and accelerating across all segments.

factors.summary: The revenue mix has consistently shifted towards higher-margin segments, particularly in the Network Software segment, with organic revenue growth of 11.3% in 2021 and 12.7% in 2022, accompanied by improving gross margins of 84.1% and 84.6% respectively.

evidence.text: In our Network Software segment, net revenues were $1,223.8 for the year ended December 31, 2021 as compared to $1,069.4 for the year ended December 31, 2020. The growth of 11.3% in organic revenues was broad-based across the segment led by our network software businesses serving the freight match, post-acute care and construction markets. Gross margin increased to 84.1% for the year ended December 31, 2021 from 83.1% for the year ended December 31, 2020, due primarily to revenue mix and operating leverage on higher organic revenues.
evidence.subsection: Year Ended December 31, 2021 Compared to Year Ended December 31, 2020

evidence.text: In our Network Software segment, net revenues were $1,378.5 for the year ended December 31, 2022 as compared to $1,223.8 for the year ended December 31, 2021. The growth of 12.7% in organic revenues was led by our network software businesses serving the freight match, life insurance, and media and entertainment markets. Gross margin increased to 84.6% for the year ended December 31, 2022 from 84.1% for the year ended December 31, 2021, due primarily to favorable revenue mix.
evidence.subsection: Year Ended December 31, 2022 Compared to Year Ended December 31, 2021

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments with improving gross margins.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins expanded from 2020 to 2021 but compressed from 2021 to 2022 due to higher costs and a shift in revenue mix.

evidence.text: Gross margin increased to 69.4% for the year ended December 31, 2021 as compared to 68.4% for the year ended December 31, 2020 due primarily to the acquisition of Vertafore and operating leverage on higher organic revenues. SG&A expenses as a percentage of revenues in the year ended December 31, 2021 increased to 42.7%, as compared to 42.2% in the year ended December 31, 2020, due primarily to higher amortization of acquired intangibles from the Vertafore and EPSi acquisitions, partially offset by operating leverage on higher organic revenues. The resulting operating margin was 26.8% in the year ended December 31, 2021 as compared to 26.2% in the year ended December 31, 2020.
evidence.subsection: Year Ended December 31, 2021 Compared to Year Ended December 31, 2020

evidence.text: Gross margin decreased to 68.8% for the year ended December 31, 2022 as compared to 69.4% for the year ended December 31, 2021 due primarily to increased headcount to support growth, and a higher mix of SaaS and professional service revenue across a number of businesses. Segment operating margin was 32.3% for the year ended December 31, 2022 as compared to 31.6% for the year ended December 31, 2021.
evidence.subsection: Year Ended December 31, 2022 Compared to Year Ended December 31, 2021

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: The margins expanded in 2021 but compressed in 2022, showing mixed performance.

factors.summary: The end markets are experiencing sustained positive momentum, with growth accelerating across a broader range of sectors in 2022 compared to 2021. Key markets driving growth include acute healthcare, government contracting, property and casualty insurance, freight match, life insurance, media and entertainment, water meter technology, and medical products, reflecting both consistency in certain sectors and expansion into new areas.

evidence.text: The growth of 9.1% in organic revenues was broad-based across the segment led by our businesses serving the government contracting, acute healthcare and legal markets.
evidence.subsection: Year Ended December 31, 2021 Compared to Year Ended December 31, 2020

evidence.text: The growth of 7.5% in organic revenues was broad-based across the segment led by our businesses serving the property and casualty insurance, acute healthcare, and government contracting markets. In our Network Software segment, net revenues were $1,378.5 for the year ended December 31, 2022 as compared to $1,223.8 for the year ended December 31, 2021. The growth of 12.7% in organic revenues was led by our network software businesses serving the freight match, life insurance, and media and entertainment markets. In our Technology Enabled Products segment, net revenues were $1,353.8 for the year ended December 31, 2022 as compared to $1,243.3 the year ended December 31, 2021. The growth of 9.8% in organic revenues was primarily due to our water meter technology business and medical products businesses.
evidence.subsection: Year Ended December 31, 2022 Compared to Year Ended December 31, 2021

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: End markets are experiencing sustained positive momentum with growth accelerating across sectors.

factors.summary: The company has been actively engaging in both acquisitions and divestitures. While the acquisitions have been successful and value-creating, the divestiture of a significant portion of its industrial businesses was a strategic move to reduce cyclicality and asset intensity.

evidence.text: Cash used in investing activities from continuing operations during 2022 was primarily for business acquisitions, most notably Frontline Education, viDesktop and MGA Systems. Cash used in investing activities from continuing operations during 2021 was primarily for business acquisitions partially offset by proceeds from the sale of a minority investment. Cash used in investing activities from continuing operations during 2020 was primarily for business acquisitions, most notably Vertafore and EPSi.
evidence.subsection: All currency amounts are in millions

evidence.text: On November 22, 2022, the Company completed the divestiture of a majority 51% equity stake in its industrial businesses, including its entire historical Process Technologies reportable segment and the industrial businesses within its historical Measurement & Analytical Solutions reportable segment, to Clayton, Dubilier & Rice, LLC.
evidence.subsection: Discontinued Operations

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Active and successful capital allocation strategies, including value-creating acquisitions and strategic divestitures.

factors.summary: Foreign exchange movements are a risk that could significantly reduce reported revenues and earnings, particularly impacting Technology Enabled Products. While the exact impact of unfavorable exchange rates is noted, the specific direction (positive or negative) is not explicitly detailed in the MDA section beyond the stated percentage impact.

evidence.text: Several of our operating companies have transactions and balances denominated in currencies other than the U.S. dollar. Most of these transactions and balances are denominated in euros, Canadian dollars, or British pounds. Sales by our operating companies whose functional currency is not the U.S. dollar represented 11% and 12% of our total net revenues for the years ended December 31, 2022 and 2021, respectively. Unfavorable changes in exchange rates between the U.S. dollar and those currencies could significantly reduce our reported revenues and earnings.
evidence.subsection: Unfavorable changes in foreign exchange rates may harm our business.

evidence.text: Foreign Exchange (1.3) (1.3) (0.9) (1.2) Organic Revenue Growth 7.5% 12.7% 9.8% 9.4% ... Gross margin decreased to 56.9% in the year ended December 31, 2022, as compared to 59.2% in the year ended December 31, 2021, due primarily to higher material, component and freight costs as our businesses navigate the widespread global supply chain challenges.
evidence.subsection: Year Ended December 31, 2022 Compared to Year Ended December 31, 2021

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Foreign exchange movements pose a significant risk to revenues and earnings.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include supply chain challenges, rising input costs, and foreign exchange risks. The company is actively managing these risks through inventory build and operational adjustments.

evidence.text: Net working capital (total current assets, excluding cash and current assets held for sale, less total current liabilities, excluding debt and current liabilities held for sale) was negative $1,053.7 at December 31, 2022 compared to negative $990.9 at December 31, 2021, due primarily to increased deferred revenue, partially offset by movements in income tax-related balances and greater inventory build associated with mitigating supply chain challenges.
evidence.subsection: All currency amounts are in millions

evidence.text: Gross margin decreased to 56.9% in the year ended December 31, 2022, as compared to 59.2% in the year ended December 31, 2021, due primarily to higher material, component and freight costs as our businesses navigate the widespread global supply chain challenges.
evidence.subsection: Year Ended December 31, 2022 Compared to Year Ended December 31, 2021

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Near-term risks include supply chain challenges, rising input costs, and foreign exchange risks.

factors.summary: The company is facing supply chain challenges, particularly in Technology Enabled Products, which have impacted gross margins due to higher material and freight costs. However, efforts to mitigate these challenges, such as increasing inventory levels, are being made.

evidence.text: Net working capital (total current assets, excluding cash and current assets held for sale, less total current liabilities, excluding debt and current liabilities held for sale) was negative $1,053.7 at December 31, 2022 compared to negative $990.9 at December 31, 2021, due primarily to increased deferred revenue, partially offset by movements in income tax-related balances and greater inventory build associated with mitigating supply chain challenges.
evidence.subsection: All currency amounts are in millions

evidence.text: Gross margin decreased to 56.9% in the year ended December 31, 2022, as compared to 59.2% in the year ended December 31, 2021, due primarily to higher material, component and freight costs as our businesses navigate the widespread global supply chain challenges.
evidence.subsection: Year Ended December 31, 2022 Compared to Year Ended December 31, 2021

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Supply chain challenges are impacting gross margins due to higher material and freight costs.
